title: Regime-gated ensemble with rank-preserving blend instead of probability-heavy calibration
summary: Recent pit-stop papers find sequence models capture long dependencies, while tree models remain strong on tabular regime interactions.
rationale: Recent pit-stop papers find sequence models capture long dependencies, while tree models remain strong on tabular regime interactions. Given ROC AUC is rank-based, a practical adjacent-competition pattern is to blend heterogeneous specialists by rank rather than aggressively calibrating probabilities, which can add ties or chase logloss instead of ranking.
implementation_hint: Train 2-3 complementary specialists: current tabular tree model, a short-sequence model over prior laps or lag features, and optionally the race-lap ranking model. Blend out-of-fold predictions by rank-average globally or with simple gates for wet vs slick, early vs late race, and first stint vs later stint. Prefer monotone meta-blends over isotonic recalibration when optimizing AUC.
expected_effect: Improves leaderboard robustness by exploiting complementary error patterns across regimes without needing a full end-to-end sequence system.
risk: Sequence models can overfit because public F1 papers often use richer telemetry and non-AUC metrics; gating rules must be chosen out-of-fold or they become another leakage path.
